summary: react-simple-utils-kit exfiltrates installer credentials and host reconnaissance to attacker-controlled endpoint at install time
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes shell commands to harvest installer-side secrets and host reconnaissance, then POSTs the results over plain HTTP to http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (a request-bin / interactsh-style attacker endpoint). Collected data includes /root/.npmrc (npm publish token), /root/.gitconfig, /root/.profile, /root/.bashrc, /root/.bunfig.toml, /proc/mounts, recursive listings of /opt /root /etc /home /space /skills, contents of /tmp files, git history and remotes, and — most damaging — the environment variables of other running processes scraped via `cat /proc/[0-9]*/environ` filtered through `grep -iE 'key|token|secret|pass|auth'`. The package's index.js is a 30-line trivial date-formatting utility used as cover; the author field is empty and the React-utility name is a generic namespace lure. The postinstall comment claims 'authorized security testing' but the package is published publicly to npm as a developer utility, which is inconsistent with that claim.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
